ddls:
CREATE TABLE "timing_parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each timing parameter */,
  "symbol_id" INTEGER /* ID of the symbol used to represent the timing parameter */,
  "parameter_name_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parameter name */,
  "min_value" TEXT /* Minimum value for the timing parameter */,
  "max_value" TEXT /* Maximum value for the timing parameter */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the unit of measurement for the timing parameter */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the timing parameter */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the timing parameter */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the timing parameter was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the timing parameter */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the timing parameter was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_timing_parameters_parameter_name_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_name_id") REFERENCES parameters ("parameter_name_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_timing_parameters_symbol_id FOREIGN KEY ("symbol_id") REFERENCES symbols ("symbol_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_timing_parameters_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES units ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "symbols" (
  "symbol_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each symbol */,
  "symbol_name" TEXT /* Name of the symbol */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the symbol */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the symbol */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the symbol was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the symbol */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the symbol was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("symbol_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "units" (
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each unit */,
  "unit_name" TEXT /* Name of the unit */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the unit */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the unit */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the unit was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the unit */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the unit was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parameters" (
  "parameter_name_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter name */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the parameter */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the parameter */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the parameter was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the parameter */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the parameter was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_name_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "timing_diagrams" (
  "diagram_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each timing diagram */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the timing parameter the diagram belongs to */,
  "diagram_file" TEXT /* File path to the timing diagram */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date when the timing diagram was uploaded */,
  "uploaded_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who uploaded the timing diagram */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the timing diagram */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the timing diagram was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("diagram_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_timing_diagrams_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES timing_parameters ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the timing parameter the report belongs to */,
  "report_file" TEXT /* File path to the report */,
  "generate_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "generated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the report */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the report was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES timing_parameters ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, quality assurance, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the user */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the timing parameter being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the timing parameter */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the timing parameter was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the access log */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the access log was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES timing_parameters ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the role */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_assignments" (
  "assignment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role assignment */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user being assigned a role */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role being assigned */,
  "assigned_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who assigned the role */,
  "assigned_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was assigned */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assignment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_role_assignments_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_role_assignments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
